text: Sociologist Georg Simmel wrote in 1903 that city life forces the mind into a defensive posture: confronted with relentless stimulation, urban dwellers develop a blasé attitude — a deliberate flattening of responsiveness — as a form of psychic self-protection. The smartphone era has urbanized the mind globally. Every person, regardless of geography, now inhabits a stimulation density that Simmel associated only with the largest metropolises. The attention economy did not merely commodify focus; it created a condition in which withdrawal from stimulation requires active effort, while submission to it requires none.
question: What did Georg Simmel call the defensive psychological posture that urban dwellers adopt against relentless stimulation?
